properties.description:
Detects potential email bombing attacks against internal mailboxes. 
The rule looks for inbound email bursts where a recipient receives 
150 or more messages within a 5-minute window. To reduce false positives 
from automated systems and bulk senders, known domains are excluded and 
the detection requires more than 20 unique sender addresses in that window.
Key logic:
EmailDirection == "Inbound"
Excludes common service domains (e.g., service-now.com, bankunited.com, fiserv.com)
Aggregates by RecipientEmailAddress in 5-minute bins
Triggers when EmailCount >= 150 and DistinctSenders > 20
Projects context fields such as DistinctDomains, SampleDomains, SampleSenders, and SampleSubjects to assist in triage
This rule is designed to surface high-confidence signs of email bombing (mass unsolicited messages intended to overwhelm a mailbox).
properties.query:
let threshold = 150;
 let window    = 5m;
 EmailEvents
 | where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
 | extend SenderDomain = tolower(SenderFromDomain)
 | where SenderDomain  !in ("service-now.com","bankunited.com","cincnotification.com","derivativeedge.com",
                 "teams.mail.microsoft.com","fiserv.com","bankunited.coupahost.com",
                 "sailpointuat.bankunited.com","now.bankunited.com")
 | summarize
     EmailCount        = count(),
     DistinctSenders   = dcount(SenderFromAddress),
     DistinctDomains   = dcount(SenderDomain),
     SampleSenders     = make_set(SenderFromAddress, 5),
     SampleDomains     = make_set(SenderDomain, 5),
     SampleSubjects    = make_set(Subject, 5)
   by RecipientEmailAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, window)
 | where EmailCount >= threshold
     and DistinctSenders > 20
 | project
     TimeGenerated,
     RecipientEmailAddress,
     EmailCount,
     DistinctSenders,
     DistinctDomains,
     SampleDomains,
     SampleSenders,
     SampleSubjects
 | order by EmailCount desc
